transcript: Each historical moment needs some context, past events that add other meanings and perspectives to the moment and the 2005 champions league final between ac milan and liverpool is only a small part of the whole story the story of how these two prestigious teams got to the point of being a kick or a save away from lifting the Champions League trophy. after winning the league title in 1990, Liverpool Football Club went through a transitional period in the following years. The prestigious club was still grasping on to the past and all the methods that made them successful. With the big announcement and formation of the Premier League in 1992, most clubs took big strides in the right direction, and Liverpool kept wondering, when will I win? As if it was a simple question of time, instead, they should have looked at themselves and asked whether they were good enough or not. And in came ex-Reds legend Graeme Souness in the beginning to introduce new training and diet methods to the club when he took over as manager in 1991. But three seasons of this experiment were enough, and the change was rejected by the fans and management. As the Reds finished in 6th, 8th, and 4th in the first three seasons of the Premier League, the club was struggling to hold on to their fame in front of the eyes of the new global fans and thus missing out on numerous bonuses and astronomical contracts and sponsorships which other clubs had received. Liverpool went back to their roots and methods of the past and instead of taking a chance on finding a foreign manager with new concepts, the club promoted Roy Evans from within, who was a big part of the club for the last couple of decades and within many capacities. With the passing of time, all what's left of the glorious Liverpool team of the 70s and 80s disappeared. Some decided to retire and others left after getting older after getting seriously injured, like in the case of John Barnes. The Reds' line-up was suddenly filled with transfer flops and local youngsters. Some youth products like Robbie Fowler, Steve McManaman and Jamie Redknapp were great, but others were far away from that. And this group of players were acting like celebrities, as they are playing for a prestigious club like Liverpool but without any titles to show for which made the Reds the best in the country this group was famously called the Spice Boys but from 1991 until 2000 the team had only won two titles which were the FA Cup in 92 and the League Cup in 95 so So with the success of Arsene Wenger at Arsenal in that period Liverpool management led by club president David Moores and new chief executive Rick Perry sought to find a new Arsene Wenger and then came ex-French national team manager Gerard Oliere in the summer of 98. The Reds clearly wanted to get with the times and take a step in the right direction, but at the same time, they were still holding on to the past, as Ollier came in and took the role of joint manager, which was a very strange job in which he was asked to do his usual managerial tasks equally with the current manager at that time, Roy Evans. And despite the strong start to this experiment, there were clear differences between the two managers. And squad member David Thompson said recently that nobody knew who was in charge and what was going on. The experiment ended after Roy Evans' resignation in November to make way for O'Leary's sole role as manager. But the damage was done as the Reds were out of contention of each tournament. Oulier made some huge changes to the team as he wanted to strengthen the culture of discipline in the club and got rid of the players that didn't stand with this approach, the captain Paul Ince being one of them. Management brought in ex-legend Phil Thompson as assistant manager to bring some discipline to the team. And the French manager had a tough job for the next couple of seasons after ending his first year in 7th position and 22 points away from the Champions United. But management were patient with the new manager and gave him plenty of time. The French manager used this time to develop some youngsters at the club like Michael Owen, Steven Gerrard and Jamie Carragher. And in the summer transfer market of 99, Gerrard Oliere focused on strengthening the defence as he signed both Samy Hubia and Stéphane Ancheu. And he also brought in Dietmar Hamann and Vladimir Smitser. It was a mini-revolution at the club. The defence was greatly improved, as despite of the injury to the attacking duo of Fowler and Owen, the defence kept the team in the European spots, along with great performances from the Czech Patrik Berger and the new January signing, Emil Husky. Boosted by that, the Reds managed to end the season in fourth and thus qualifying to the UEFA Cup. The manager reaped the fruits of this revolution in the following year, which is considered one of the most exciting seasons in the Reds' history. From February of 2001 till the end of the season, the team took part in several fateful matches. Firstly in the League Cup final against Birmingham City, where the two teams fought it out until extra time and won two penalties to settle the match, secondly the FA Cup Final, where Owen and Liverpool came back to win against Arsenal in the dying minutes, and thirdly when they got all the way to the UEFA Cup Final after winning against Roma, Porto and Barcelona on their way to the final, and the Reds took part in a crazy match. As the match got to extra time after a 4-4 draw, and was sealed by golden goal which an Alevesh defender scored inadvertently the Reds managed to win a unique treble which could be labeled as the cup treble and that season introduced us to a new champion and that was a young Steven Gerrard who gave so much to the team and won the PFA Young Player of the Year award. And thus, after years of drought, Liverpool won several titles in a single season. And now it was finally the time to win the title that eluded the Reds for many years, and that was the Premier League. league. With the Reds qualifying to the Champions League for the first time since the Heysel disaster in 1985, it was obvious that there were big ambitions for the upcoming season. It's true that the Reds let go of a club legend like Roby Fowler to Leeds United, after the relationship with his French manager took a very sour turn, but the team compensated well in the summer and January windows. However, with these ambitions, there must have been added pressures mounting on the French manager, and in a match against Leeds in October, he was rushed to the hospital at half-time, and it turned out he had some serious heart issues. Assistant manager Phil Thompson took over for five months before the Frenchman returned without the knowledge of the fans in a crucial match against Italian champions Roma. The reception by the fans for the French manager was truly touching. And the team managed to win and get through to the quarter-finals of the Champions League, as Gerard Houllier was quoted saying that the team was only 10 matches away from achieving greatness. That beautiful European night against Roma seemed to be the beginning of something special at Anfield. Instead, it proved to be the beginning of the end for the Reds and Uliere in the years to come. The European journey stopped after the loss against Leverkusen and the Reds settled for second place behind Arsenal in the league. And bad decisions started to hinder the development of the squad, especially after the team failed to make Nicolas Anelka's loan a permanent deal, as the club replaced him with El Hadji Diouf and Salif Diouf, the Senegalese duo. The transfer business was not only bad, but it affected the chemistry of the team as well, as the team were quickly out of the title race after failing to win any Premier League match for two months. They also crashed out of the group stages of the Champions League after losing the two legs against an exciting Valencia team led by the Spaniard Rafa Benitez. The Reds failed to win in their last faithful league match against Chelsea, which ended their chances of playing Champions League football next season. The win in the League Cup final against United was the only bright spot of an average season, but management eventually stood by their manager despite tension from some Liverpool fans. The behaviour of the French manager began to alarm some of the players, as Dudik mentioned in his autobiography that Oliere had become suspicious of his own squad and he felt that his players were playing badly to get him fired. And the Reds continued to struggle despite bringing in the Aussie star Harry Kuehl from Leeds United as they were far away from challenging the other teams in the division but managed to end the season in a Champions League spot. However, it was not enough to keep the French manager at the club, as he left by mutual consent. And maybe his health was one of the reasons for his departure. If people decide that I'm not the right man for the right place, so what? I'm not important. I'm not here for myself. I'm here for the club. at the end of the 80s and the beginning of the 90s, there was only one club which had its name repeated in several different podiums. Whether it was Arrigo Sacchi who led the team or Fabio Capello, Milan had achieved everything possible for a European club. The first 10 years for Silvio Berlusconi in the footballing world and as president of AC Milan were no doubt a huge success. But each and every successful project must go through a transitional period, a period of reshuffling the assets, a period of reviving the team again. And for Milan, that period came after Capello's departure in 96 to Madrid. And throughout the assets, a period of reviving the team again. And for Milan, that period came after Capello's departure in 96 to Madrid. And throughout the summer, the Rossoneri had done numerous big changes, as Berlusconi's focus shifted from Milan to his political plans and responsibilities, as he handed over his club-related duties to vice-president Adriano Gagliani and general manager Arriedo Braida. The beginnings with Gagliani were no doubt a train wreck. The focus was on the quantity of transfers instead of the quality, and Rossoneri replaced Capello with the Uruguayan manager Oscar Tabarez after his successful time at Cagliari. The transfers didn't gel quickly, and management decided swiftly to fire the new manager in December after a bad run of results. The team then tried to revive the glory days when they brought back Enrico Sacchi, who had just left his position in the Italian national team. Sacchi couldn't do much to steer the ship as he was destined for failure, as the season ended badly, with Milan in 11th position, the worst showing in the Berlusconi era for the Rossoneri. That position more or less drove club legends Tassotti and Bereze to finally retire. The goals of the Rossoneri were not clear, neither the philosophy was set, as Capello came back again in the following season to bring back any kind of success to this team But all the Italian manager's attempts ended in failure And the transfers failed again and the script was repeated as evident on the field With Milan losing heavily against Roma And for the second year in a row, the Rossoneri failed to capture a European spot As they settled for 10th position Massimo Ambrosini said recently that most of the Milan players at that time We'll be right back. He brought with him the top goalscorer of the league, Oliver Behoef, and the Danish wing-back, Helvig. Despite Milan not being one of the favourites for the title that year, the Rossoneri strongly competed throughout the season, and Zaccherone used his favourite and trusted shape, the 3-4-3 with big success, and with Bihoff and Leonardo's crucial goals, and the runs of the wing-back Juli, and the threat of Giorgioie, the Rossoneri managed to surprise everybody and win the title in the last match after Lazio's historic stumbles in the last weeks, Gagliani and Braidas decisions in the market were getting much better, but the results on the field were not reflecting that. And after years of speculation, in came the Ukrainian striker Andriy Shevchenko to Milan in the summer of 1999, and with him Gennaro Gattuso and the Brazilian wing-back Serginho. And despite the brilliance of the new Capocaniere of the league Shevchenko, Zaccheroni failed to bring back the fire of last season as their European hopes were shattered from the group stages as they settled for last place. The Rossoneri failed to keep up the pressure on Lazio and Juve in the league and settled for third position and without a single title to show for. The huge spending continued next summer and with every lira being invested in the club, the pressure increased on the manager Zaccheroni, especially after UEFA announced that the Champions League final will be held in the San Siro. And the manager contracts at Milan are usually tied to results, so after crashing out of the second group stages in the Champions League in March, Berlusconi had enough, as he interfered with club decisions and fired Zaccheroni. Then came ex-Milan legend Cesare Maldini as caretaker, and with him Tassotti as an assistant, but the new staff failed to capture a Champions League spot for next season, but they managed to win historically in the derby della Madonnina by 6 goals to nil. It was a result never repeated in history since the new Serie A was formed in 1929. The Turkish manager Fatih Terim was chosen after he managed to achieve great results against Milan with Galatasaray in the previous seasons. Galliani wanted a manager with great charisma and used positive tactics to put the stars of the team in good use especially after the Rossoneri paid a record number in the summer to bring Rui Costa, Andrea Pirlo, Philippe Bonzaghi and other notable players. Milan spending in the summer of 2001 is considered the most expensive in Berlusconi's era so expectations were through the roof and the result Fatih Terim was fired in November after many bad results and because of the language barrier, along with the difficulty in explaining the philosophy and tactics to the players in detail. On the same day, Galliani took a quick decision and called Carlo Ancelotti as he was going back to Parma for a second stint. And Gagliani restated Carlo's promise before he retired, if the Rossoneri called you to coach the team, you'll give them priority over everybody else. Ancelotti was considered a gamble at that time, a manager who only won the Intertoto Cup with Juve despite boosting several stars and he'll come to a team which paid big sums of money but failed to achieve anything yet. Carlo didn't change the course of the season. He still needed time to get the best out of the players in his disposal. Although the Rossoneri managed to get a Champions League spot for next season, the competition which is still Berlusconi's number one priority. That's why the team invested heavily again the following summer and brought Alessandro Nesta from Lazio, Seedorf from Inter and Rivaldo in a free transfer. It could be stated that the most important signing done by management was the signing of the Belgian doctor Jean-Pierre Mersiman, who is the founder of Milan Lab. After Fernando Redondo's injury in 2000, which made him miss two years of football, management sought to decrease the risk of injuries in general. And the Lab will help mitigate these injuries, and also help the players get the best out of themselves on the field. On a purely personal level, Carlo Ancelotti created numerous relationships with the players, and one of his first important decisions as manager was to play Andrea Pirlo as a deep-lying midfielder, as whenever Rui Costa was starting, Pirlo had minimal chances in the starting lineup. At that time, everybody thought that Pirlo was more of an advanced playmaker, but Ancelotti suggested the change in position in August of 2002, as Pirlo agreed on the change, and said that Carlo Mazzoni used to play the Italian youngster in that same position when he played for Brescia. With Shevchenko's injury in the beginning of the season and in the group stages of the Champions League, Milan travelled to face the tough opponent Deportivo La Carugna at the Riazor Stadium. On that day, Ancelotti's favourite formation was born, the 4-3-2-1, where the three-roaming duo of Rui Costa and Rivaldo would play behind a sole strike-up, which in that case was Zanzaghi. So when the Rossoneri won by four goals to nil, all the challengers immediately knew that Milan was one of the favourites for the title. And despite the inconsistent form in the league and Shevchenko's slow return from injury, the Rossoneri managed to get all the way to the Champions League final after defeating their city rivals Internazionale in a very tense European semi-final. They will face Juve in a Champions League final with an Italian taste, and there were several headlines for the match, Ancelotti against his old employer and El Piero against his nemesis Inzaghi. With Nedved's absence, Lippi entered the match with caution, as Ancelotti changed the shape and entered with caution as well. What resulted from that was a final without excitement and without goals after extra time. As Cruyff was quoted saying that UEFA should not present the title to anyone, this is not football. But Milan managed to hold on and win the title after the Brazilian keeper Dida saved some crucial penalties and after a successful penalty from Shevchenko. Three days later, they won the Coppa Italia against Roma to end the season in big success. A clever management team is the one that strengthens the squad in the middle of success. So when ex-Rossoneri Leonardo praised a young Brazilian called Kaká and said that he's a player that could be a cornerstone of a future project, Gagliani didn't hesitate and signed with the young Brazilian immediately, and with him the national team captain Cafu, who was recommended by the doctor Merseiman. Kaká quickly proved that he wasn't only the future, but the present as well. And the evidence of that was when he took Rivaldo's place in the starting lineup, as Rivaldo decided to cancel his contract and leave for Olympiacos. Kaká was a primary weapon in Ancelotti's lineup and had freedom to roam on the field and produced great performances throughout the season. He also provided great link-up play with Shevchenko, Inzaghi and Thomasson. So finally and after numerous attempts with Parma, Juve and Milan, Ancelotti managed to win the league title after a crucial win against rivals Roma at the San Siro. The European trip ended with a historic remonta, whose victim was the Rossoneri, as after winning by four goals to one in the first leg of the quarter-finals, Milan suffered a huge defeat after Deportivo La Carugna's historic comeback in the Riazor Stadium. The exit was a huge surprise, but with this group of players and that manager, Milan is still without a doubt the number one favorite for next season milan and liverpool entered the season in completely different circumstances the rossoneri won the league title and the champions league in the last two years they They are supported by a stable management team, and they have a complete squad with room for improvement, depending on Kaká's development as a player and their new transfers. It's extremely hard to share the same view when talking about the Reds, a club that is still without a manager and in danger of losing their best players. The search for a new manager started in March of 2004, when chief executive Rick Perry watched the triumph of Porto led by Jose Mourinho against United at the Old Trafford. Management were impressed by Jose, and the Portuguese showed interest in the job of managing the Reds, as his agent has mentioned to Perry. But Mourinho's extravagant celebration that day gave a bad impression to the Reds' management, and the chief executive mentioned recently that Mourinho is no doubt a great manager, but did he really represent the club's values at that time? Andrea Pirlo. It's Pirlo. He's got it. Terrific stop. Pirlo tried to deceive him. But he's got a chance here to give Liverpool a two-goal advantage, Gibral Sissi, 2-0! Harman and Sissi, yes, Serginho and Pirlo, no, Thomason, he's scored, a man off the mark and you can hear and all the pin drop silence, reset scores, Liverpool are at at match point can hit the back of the net threw himself down to his ride and just managed to divert it around the post the brazilian caca dudek with a bit of the grovel on wobbly legs 2-2 in the, Vladimir Smica can maintain that advantage, it's Smica, match point Liverpool, great penalty that is, Andrei Shevchenko, who scored the decisive penalty in the shootout at Old Trafford two years ago, which won the Champions League for Milan. Must score. He must score. It's ended. The European Cup is returning to England and to Anfield. Liverpool are champions of Europe again. The most extraordinary night The football Has a most extraordinary finish Liverpool have their hands on the European Cup again And this time it's for Keats Transcription by ESO. Translation by —

timestamps.text: Each historical moment needs some context, past events that add other meanings and perspectives to the moment and the
2005 champions league final between ac milan and liverpool is only a small part of the whole story
the story of how these two prestigious teams got to the point of being a kick
or a save away from lifting the Champions League trophy.

after winning the league title in 1990, Liverpool Football Club went through a transitional period in the following years. The prestigious club was still grasping on to the past and all the methods that made them successful. With the big announcement and formation of the Premier League in 1992, most clubs took big strides in the right direction, and Liverpool kept wondering, when will I win? As if it was a simple question of time, instead, they should have looked at themselves and asked whether they were good enough or not. And in came ex-Reds legend Graeme Souness in the beginning to introduce new training and diet methods to the club when he took over as manager in 1991. But three seasons of this experiment were enough, and the change was rejected by the fans and management. As the Reds finished in 6th, 8th, and 4th in the first three seasons of the Premier League, the club was struggling to hold on to their fame in front of the eyes of the new global fans and thus missing out on numerous bonuses and astronomical contracts and sponsorships which other clubs had received. Liverpool went back to their roots and methods of the past and instead of taking a chance on finding a foreign manager with new concepts, the club promoted Roy Evans from within, who was a big part of the club for the last couple of decades and within many capacities. With the passing of time, all what's left of the glorious Liverpool team of the 70s and 80s disappeared. Some decided to retire and others left after getting older after getting seriously injured, like in the case of John Barnes.
The Reds' line-up was suddenly filled with transfer flops and local youngsters.
Some youth products like Robbie Fowler, Steve McManaman and Jamie Redknapp were great, but others were far away from that.

And this group of players were acting like celebrities, as they are playing for a prestigious club like Liverpool but without any titles to show for which made the Reds the best in the country this group was famously called the Spice Boys but from 1991 until 2000 the team had only won two titles which were the FA Cup in 92 and the League Cup in 95 so So with the success of Arsene Wenger at Arsenal in that period Liverpool management led by club president David Moores and new chief executive Rick Perry sought to find a new Arsene Wenger and then came ex-French national team manager Gerard Oliere in the summer of 98. The Reds clearly wanted to get with the times and take a step in the right direction, but at the same time, they were still holding on to the past,
as Ollier came in and took the role of joint manager, which was a very strange job in which
he was asked to do his usual managerial tasks equally with the current manager at that time,

Roy Evans. And despite the strong start to this experiment, there were clear differences between the two managers. And squad member David Thompson said recently that nobody knew who was in charge and what was going on. The experiment ended after Roy Evans' resignation in November to make way for O'Leary's sole role as manager. But the damage was done as the Reds were out of contention of each tournament. Oulier made some huge changes to the team as he wanted to strengthen the culture of discipline in the club and got rid of the players that didn't stand with this approach, the captain Paul Ince being one of them. Management brought in ex-legend Phil Thompson as assistant manager

to bring some discipline to the team. And the French manager had a tough job for the next couple of seasons after ending his first year in 7th position and 22 points away from the Champions United. But management were patient with the new manager and gave him plenty of time. The French manager used this time to develop some youngsters at the club like Michael Owen, Steven Gerrard and Jamie Carragher. And in the summer transfer market of 99, Gerrard Oliere focused on strengthening the defence as he signed both Samy Hubia and Stéphane Ancheu. And he also brought in Dietmar Hamann and Vladimir Smitser. It was a mini-revolution at the club. The defence was greatly improved, as despite of the injury to the attacking duo of Fowler and Owen, the defence kept the team in the European spots, along with great performances from the Czech Patrik Berger and the new January signing, Emil Husky. Boosted by that, the Reds managed to end the season in fourth and thus qualifying to the
UEFA Cup.
The manager reaped the fruits of this revolution in the following year, which is considered
one of the most exciting seasons in the Reds' history.
From February of 2001 till the end of the season, the team took part in several fateful

matches. Firstly in the League Cup final against Birmingham City, where the two teams fought it out until extra time and won two penalties to settle the match, secondly the FA Cup Final, where Owen and Liverpool came back to win against Arsenal in the dying minutes, and thirdly when they got all the

way to the UEFA Cup Final after winning against Roma, Porto and Barcelona on their way to the final, and the Reds took part in a crazy match. As the match got to extra time after a 4-4 draw, and was sealed by golden goal which an Alevesh defender scored inadvertently the Reds managed to win a unique treble which could be labeled as the cup treble and that season introduced us to a new champion and that was a young Steven Gerrard who gave so much to the team and won the PFA Young Player of the Year award.

And thus, after years of drought, Liverpool won several titles in a single season. And now it was finally the time to win the title that eluded the Reds for many years, and that was the Premier League. league. With the Reds qualifying to the Champions League for the first time since the Heysel disaster in 1985, it was obvious that there were big ambitions for the upcoming season. It's true that the Reds let go of a club legend like Roby Fowler to Leeds United, after the relationship with his French manager took a very sour turn, but the team compensated well in the summer and January windows. However, with these ambitions, there must have been added pressures mounting on the French manager, and in a match against Leeds in October, he was rushed to the hospital at half-time, and it turned out he had some serious heart issues. Assistant manager Phil Thompson took over for five months before the Frenchman returned without the knowledge of the fans in a crucial match against Italian champions Roma. The reception by the fans for the French manager was truly touching. And the team managed to win and get through to the quarter-finals of the Champions League, as Gerard Houllier was quoted saying that the team was only 10 matches away from achieving greatness. That beautiful European night against Roma seemed to be the beginning of something special at Anfield. Instead, it proved to be the beginning of the end for the Reds and

Uliere in the years to come. The European journey stopped after the loss against Leverkusen and the Reds settled for second place behind Arsenal in the league. And bad decisions started to hinder the development of the squad, especially after the team failed to make Nicolas Anelka's loan a permanent deal, as the club replaced him with El Hadji Diouf and Salif Diouf, the Senegalese duo. The transfer business was not only bad, but it affected the chemistry of the team as well, as the team were quickly out of the title race after failing to win any Premier League match for two months. They also crashed out of the group stages of the Champions League after losing the two legs against an exciting Valencia team led by the Spaniard Rafa Benitez. The Reds failed to win in their last faithful league match against Chelsea, which ended their chances of playing Champions League football next season. The win in the League Cup final against United was the only bright spot of an average season, but management eventually stood by their manager despite tension from some Liverpool fans. The behaviour of the French manager began to alarm some of the players, as Dudik mentioned in his autobiography that Oliere had become suspicious of his own squad and he felt that his players were playing badly to get him fired. And the Reds continued to struggle despite bringing in the Aussie star Harry Kuehl from Leeds United as they were far away from challenging the other teams in the division but managed to end the season in a Champions League spot. However, it was not enough to keep the French manager at the club, as he left by mutual consent.
And maybe his health was one of the reasons for his departure.

If people decide that I'm not the right man for the right place, so what? I'm not important. I'm not here for myself. I'm here for the club. at the end of the 80s and the beginning of the 90s, there was only one club which had its name repeated in several different podiums. Whether it was Arrigo Sacchi who led the team or Fabio Capello, Milan had achieved everything possible for a European club. The first 10
years for Silvio Berlusconi in the footballing world and as president of AC Milan were no doubt
a huge success. But each and every successful project must go through a transitional period,
a period of reshuffling the assets, a period of reviving the team again. And for Milan,

that period came after Capello's departure in 96 to Madrid. And throughout the assets, a period of reviving the team again. And for Milan, that period came after Capello's departure in 96 to Madrid. And throughout the summer, the Rossoneri had done numerous big changes, as Berlusconi's focus shifted from Milan to his political plans and responsibilities, as he handed over his club-related duties to vice-president Adriano Gagliani and general manager Arriedo Braida. The beginnings with Gagliani were no doubt a train wreck. The focus was on the quantity of transfers instead of the quality, and Rossoneri replaced Capello with the Uruguayan manager Oscar Tabarez after his successful time at Cagliari. The transfers didn't gel quickly, and management decided swiftly to fire the new manager in December after a bad run of results. The team then tried to revive the glory days when they brought back Enrico Sacchi, who had just left his position in the Italian national team. Sacchi couldn't do much to steer the ship as he was destined for failure, as the season ended badly, with Milan in 11th position, the worst showing in the Berlusconi era for the Rossoneri. That position more or less drove club legends Tassotti and Bereze to finally retire. The goals of the Rossoneri were not clear, neither the philosophy was set, as Capello came back again in the following season to bring back any kind of success to this team
But all the Italian manager's attempts ended in failure
And the transfers failed again and the script was repeated as evident on the field
With Milan losing heavily against Roma
And for the second year in a row, the Rossoneri failed to capture a European spot
As they settled for 10th position
Massimo Ambrosini said recently that most of the Milan players at that time

We'll be right back. Leary failed to capture a European spot as they settled for 10th position. Massimo Ambrosini said recently that most of the Milan players at that time were not motivated like in the past and Capello had an extremely tough job. That's why Galliani sought to bring a new face to the club and in came Alberto Zaccherone from Udinese after successfully securing third position last season. He brought with him the top goalscorer of the league, Oliver Behoef, and the Danish wing-back, Helvig. Despite Milan not being one of the favourites for the title that year, the Rossoneri strongly competed throughout the season, and Zaccherone used his favourite and trusted shape, the 3-4-3 with big success, and with Bihoff and Leonardo's crucial goals,
and the runs of the wing-back Juli, and the threat of Giorgioie, the Rossoneri managed
to surprise everybody and win the title in the last match after Lazio's historic stumbles

in the last weeks, Gagliani and Braidas decisions in the market were getting much better, but the results on the field were not reflecting that. And after years of speculation, in came the Ukrainian striker Andriy Shevchenko to Milan in the summer of 1999, and with him Gennaro Gattuso and the Brazilian wing-back Serginho. And despite the brilliance of the new Capocaniere of the league Shevchenko, Zaccheroni failed to bring back the fire of last season as their European hopes were shattered from the group stages as they settled for last place. The Rossoneri failed to keep up the pressure on Lazio and Juve in the league and settled for third position and without a single title to show for. The huge spending continued next summer and with every lira being invested in the club, the pressure increased on the manager Zaccheroni, especially

after UEFA announced that the Champions League final will be held in the San Siro. And the manager contracts at Milan are usually tied to results, so after crashing out of the second group stages in the Champions League in March, Berlusconi had enough, as he interfered with club decisions and fired Zaccheroni. Then came ex-Milan legend Cesare Maldini as caretaker, and with him Tassotti as an assistant, but the new staff failed to capture a Champions League spot for next season, but they managed to win historically in the derby della Madonnina by 6 goals to nil. It was a result never repeated in history since the new Serie A was formed in 1929. The Turkish manager Fatih Terim was chosen after he managed to achieve great results against Milan with Galatasaray in the previous seasons. Galliani wanted a manager with great charisma and used positive tactics to put the stars of the team in good use especially after the Rossoneri paid a record number in the summer to bring Rui Costa, Andrea Pirlo, Philippe Bonzaghi and other notable players. Milan spending in the summer of 2001 is considered the most expensive in Berlusconi's era so expectations were through the roof and the result Fatih Terim was fired in November after many bad results and because of the language barrier, along with the difficulty in explaining the philosophy and tactics to the players in detail. On the same day, Galliani took a quick decision and called Carlo Ancelotti as he was going back to Parma for a second stint. And Gagliani restated Carlo's promise before he retired, if the Rossoneri called you to coach the team, you'll give them priority over everybody else. Ancelotti was considered a gamble at that time, a manager who only won the Intertoto Cup with Juve despite boosting several stars and he'll come to a team which paid big sums of money but failed to achieve anything yet. Carlo didn't change the course of the season. He still needed time to get the best out of the players in his disposal. Although the Rossoneri managed to get a Champions League spot for next season, the competition which is still Berlusconi's number one priority. That's why the team invested heavily again the following summer and brought Alessandro Nesta from Lazio, Seedorf from Inter and Rivaldo in a free transfer. It could be stated that the most important signing done by management was the signing of the Belgian doctor Jean-Pierre Mersiman, who is the founder of Milan Lab. After Fernando Redondo's injury in 2000, which made him miss two years of football, management sought to decrease the risk of injuries in general. And the Lab will help mitigate these injuries, and also help the players get the best out of themselves on the field. On a purely personal level, Carlo Ancelotti created numerous relationships with the players,
and one of his first important decisions as manager was to play Andrea Pirlo as a deep-lying midfielder,
as whenever Rui Costa was starting, Pirlo had minimal chances in the starting lineup.
At that time, everybody thought that Pirlo was more of an advanced playmaker,

but Ancelotti suggested the change in position in August of 2002, as Pirlo agreed on the change, and said that Carlo Mazzoni used to play the Italian youngster in that same position when he played for Brescia. With Shevchenko's injury in the beginning of the season and in the group stages of the Champions League, Milan travelled to face the tough opponent Deportivo La Carugna at the Riazor Stadium. On that day, Ancelotti's favourite formation was born, the 4-3-2-1, where the three-roaming duo of Rui Costa and Rivaldo would play behind a sole strike-up, which in that case was Zanzaghi. So when the Rossoneri won by four goals to nil, all the challengers immediately knew that Milan was one of the favourites for the title. And despite the inconsistent form in the league and Shevchenko's slow return from injury, the Rossoneri managed to get all the way to the Champions League final after defeating their city rivals Internazionale in a very tense European semi-final. They will face Juve in a Champions League final with an Italian taste, and there were several headlines for the match, Ancelotti against his old employer and El Piero against his nemesis Inzaghi. With Nedved's absence, Lippi entered the match with caution, as Ancelotti changed the shape and entered with caution as well.
What resulted from that was a final without excitement and without goals after extra time.
As Cruyff was quoted saying that UEFA should not present the title to anyone, this is not football.
But Milan managed to hold on and win the title after the Brazilian keeper Dida saved some crucial penalties

and after a successful penalty from Shevchenko. Three days later, they won the Coppa Italia against Roma to end the season in big success. A clever management team is the one that strengthens the squad in the middle of success. So when ex-Rossoneri Leonardo praised a young Brazilian called Kaká and said that he's a player that could be a cornerstone of a future project, Gagliani didn't hesitate and signed with the young Brazilian immediately, and with him the national team captain Cafu, who was recommended by the doctor Merseiman. Kaká quickly proved that he wasn't only the future, but the present as well. And the evidence of that was when he took Rivaldo's place in the starting lineup, as Rivaldo decided to cancel his contract and leave for Olympiacos. Kaká was a primary weapon in Ancelotti's lineup and had freedom to roam on the field and produced great performances throughout the season. He also provided great link-up play with Shevchenko, Inzaghi and Thomasson. So finally and after numerous attempts with Parma, Juve and Milan, Ancelotti managed to win the league title after a crucial win against rivals Roma at the San Siro. The European trip ended with a historic remonta, whose victim was the Rossoneri, as after winning by four goals to one in the first leg of the quarter-finals, Milan suffered a huge defeat after Deportivo La Carugna's historic comeback in the Riazor Stadium.
The exit was a huge surprise, but with this group of players and that manager,

Milan is still without a doubt the number one favorite for next season milan and liverpool entered the season in completely different circumstances the rossoneri won the league title and the champions league in the last two years they They are supported by a stable management team, and they have a complete squad with room for improvement, depending on Kaká's development as a player and their new transfers. It's extremely hard to share the same view when talking about the Reds, a club that is still without a manager and in danger of losing their best players. The search for a new manager started in March of 2004, when chief executive Rick Perry watched the triumph of Porto led by Jose Mourinho against United at the Old Trafford. Management were impressed by Jose, and the Portuguese showed interest in the job of managing the Reds, as his agent has mentioned to Perry. But Mourinho's extravagant celebration that day gave a bad impression to the Reds' management, and the chief executive mentioned recently that Mourinho is no doubt a great manager, but did he really represent the club's values at that time? That's why Perry and the chairman David Moores decided to bring in the Spanish manager Rafa Benitez, the man who challenged the historic duopoly of Barcelona and Madrid in the Spanish league, winning La Liga twice and the UEFA Cup last year with Valencia. But the Spaniard had a very tough job in front of him. The captain Steven Gerrard was really close to signing with Chelsea and Michael Owen was thinking of Real Madrid and the club was reportedly for sale as well but at the same time management ensured that Benitez will be given plenty of time to build his own project and after returning from the national team Gerrard decided to stay whereas Michael Owen left Anfield for the Bernabeu. With Owen's departure the Reds brought in the Spanish duo of Luis Garcia and Xabi Alonso. And also the French striker, Gibril Sissi. And in the other corner, Milan brought in the tough defender, Yabstam, and the Argentine striker, Crespo, on loan from Chelsea. To qualify to the group stages of the Champions League, Liverpool had to win a tough match against the Austrian side Grazer in the qualifying rounds. The draw had the Reds in a group containing Monaco, Deportivo and Olympiacos. Milan was in a tough group with Barcelona, Shakhtar Donetsk and Celtic. After a win and a loss against Barcelona in the group headliner. The Rossoneri managed to successfully
win their group after the Blaugrana lost points in the other matches. The Reds on the other
hand were in big trouble as they have only accumulated 7 points from 5 matches in the
group and the Reds had to face their direct competition Olympiacos in the last match at
Anfield and nothing other than a win by two clear goals
would hand them qualification. And Sinema, on goal, has made an immediate impact.
Inside two minutes from being sent off.
Nunez, and in from Mela.
Only been on the pitch a matter of moments.

Mela, lovely cushion header, but Gerrard! Oh, you beauty! What a strong start to their Serie A season, and the exceptional performances of the Ukrainian Shevchenko handed him the prestigious Ballon d'Or in December. On the flip side, Rafa Benitez was struggling to adjust to the Premier League, as the team didn't possess a great striker and were suffering many defeats. One catastrophic defeat against relegation-fighting Southampton was so bad that ex-legend Alan Hansen described it as the worst defeat since he retired. Quickly, the rumours linking Gerrard to Chelsea were back, and Gerrard mentioned recently in a documentary that Benitez didn't rate him as much as he thought he should rate him. The Spaniard's failure of building a personal relationship with Gerrard could eventually push the English star into leaving. But the manager successfully steered the ship, especially in the other knockout competitions. And despite Xabi Alonso being sidelined for three months because of injury, the Reds got to the League Cup final to face their rivals Chelsea, the team that was aggressively pursuing their captain, Steven Gerrard. The ironic thing was that Gerrard scored a crucial own goal, and everyone thought that this was not Gerrard's first and last goal for Chelsea. The crucial Champions League spots were completely unattainable for the Reds and now the focus was solely on the knockout stages of the Champions League. The Rossoneri, on the other hand, kept their lead in Serie A, until a fateful match against Juve at the San Siro, where both teams were level on points going into the match. A beautiful assist by Del Piero and quick thinking by Trezeguet sealed this important match for the Bianconeri, and the Rossoneri's stumbles in the last weeks handed the Scudetto to Juve, and similar to the Reds, Milan were now focusing on the Champions League. In the round of 16, the Reds took revenge against Bayer Leverkusen and produced an unexpected attacking display as they won by six goals to two after two legs. The Rossoneri defeated the United after a crisp goal at Old Trafford
and another goal by the Argentinian in the San Siro.
And the last round positioned the two teams under great pressure. And it's certain to be one of those special European nights here at Anfield. Un saluto da Fabio Caressa e Dette D'Ergomi.
Ci siamo.
Fammi Ailovic!
Guardate che ha tolto. Fa un giro per il posto più vicino.

E c'è il Piano! La forza di un saluto! Vamiajlovic, look what he's done he makes a run to the near post as well and there is Uppia Uppia, 10 minutes gone Luis Garcia, what an effort what a goal, what a goal by Luis Garcia here goes Niedved and Ibrahimovic against the post Gerrard, Niedved, Del Piero, buon segno il 19 anni di Casso con un segno invariabile Paveiron, Dida ancora il pallone arriva poi per la conclusione ancora Dida ora Pirlo per Stade, Stam 1-0 Paras
la verso Stam
e c'è il 2-0
Dondry, Shevchenko
Seedorf trova ancora Shevchenko on the left. It's all over. Anfield, Liverpool have beaten Juventus. 2-0 for Milan. That's quite evident. He's gotten away with it, they haven't seen it.
But here he is, and he's missed a glorious opportunity.
That's a good hit and Tito is scrambling.
This is Shevchenko! A strike of staggering power.

We'll be right back. This is Shevchenko. A strike of staggering power. Now Xabi Alonso. What a lovely ball for Baros. And Baros is in. What a miss. Cruz has played it in for Martins. He's got away from Nesta. And Dida touched it onto his near post. Gone for the far post. Oh, it hit the post. And Dudek scrambles it away. And it's a corner. It's a corner. And Dida's lost that one. And Inter do have a goal back today. No, they don't. Cambiasso is absolutely furious. The referee's looking at his watch. There's the whistle. Liverpool have done it. What a fantastic achievement by the men from Antwerp. And that is just not safe. He's got to get out of that penalty area. And one of them has hit him.
Suddenly the state of the football match is in complete irrelevance.
It is crazy for Vans and Internazionale in their own ground,
in what up to this point had been a perfectly well
contested derby game
I think a team with one of the best managers in the world and some of the best players in

this country, a team with glorious pastimes that everybody respects a little bit more. Liverpool who can bribe by Rafa Benitez. This is a side who are not in the habit of losing. And it's not easy. One of the best organised defensive teams in the world to create. What we try to do is to create our chances through football. I think people all over the world saw some testing game Maros, oh, great header, fingertip save, Peter Cech. And now Farfan, and this is Farfan, he scrapes his shot horribly. Joe Cole in there, and Lampard, this is an absolute sit-up, still dropped by Jones, Chelsea, and good Janssen, couldn't quite reach it. Here is Kaká. Here is Shevchenko, onside, inevitably scoring. He just always does. Look at the eyes, look at the determination. Only one thing on his mind. Luis Garcia, clever touch, Gerrard. Now then, Chiberelle Cissé. And Lise had a pop, and Didier has lost it. And it's fortunate that it's spanned kindly for him. And feed in Farfad, and feed in Park! And Sivčík has a terrific take, it's a splendid effort, it's a brilliant save. Chance here, Risa! Csík saves and he couldn't find the corner of the net. And it reached, Thomas on! And that makes all the difference! Xabi Alonso is out of the second leg. It is over. Liverpool have got a goalless draw in the first leg of the semi-final. I think we can score goals. I think we can get a result. I think it's a completely different game for them. One thing is to come and play for a 0-0, another thing is to play and get the result, and I'm very confident we are going to the final. It's a great team, a well-organized team, I think they want to go to the final, like we do. Welcome to Eindhoven where the pressure is on They have the most expensive team in the world
The players, but we have our supporters
We have the balance now
Welcome to Anfield
On a massive night for English and yes, European football
We are not surrendering that easy
Let's go and give them another hard time
Gus Hiddick trying to mastermind the unlikely tonight
We'll be right back. We are not surrendering that easy. Let's go and give them another hard time.
Jus Hiddick trying to mastermind the unlikely tonight.
Jonad Eriksson into Steven Gerrard.
Flick through, Beresia! Can he get a touch? Was that a foul?
Luis Garcia is there as well. That might well have crossed the line anyway.

He's been given. Liverpool are ahead. What a start. Jason Park. Now Benegor of Hesseling. And Jason Park is there again to finish it. That bobbles delivery and Benegor of Hesseling's header off the bar. Luis Garcia, Lampard, Cole is in here, and Pio Lee, he's done well to pick up the cross, and the header is in from Cocu. He leaves it to Lampard and a wonderful save by Dudek, it's a mistake by Ricardo Carvalho, and Cissé's shot. Here we are, goodness me this is tense,. It really is. It is back up. Ambrosini! When it matters, Milan deliver. Kocku! Maybe they aren't done, and it is the last touch of the evening. Place it forward, Terry is forward, Ke Caseman is in there. Dunick didn't win it properly. Good job, Ted. Oh, my goodness me. Can you believe that? Can you believe that? Right at the death. Any second now. They've played over the six minutes. There it is. The return of the Reds. The glory days are back. They're in the Champions League final. Jose Mourinho's grip on the European... The
Ataturk Olympic Stadium in Istanbul will host the European final, and away from all the

I'll see you next time. The Ataturk Olympic Stadium in Istanbul will host the European final. And away from all the public transportation problems and UEFA's abject preparations, this is a match that will bring together two of the most prestigious European clubs, as the two teams have won this award in 10 occasions in total. But in reality, and at that time, there were big differences. 20 years had passed since Liverpool had last got to a European Cup final, and within that period, Milan won the title in four separate occasions. And when we look at the two lineups, we notice that Milan's squad has 13 Champions League titles to their name, Whereas the Liverpool squad had gotten to the final for the very first time The two press conferences before the match also highlighted those differences Carlo Ancelotti mentioned that we are less nervous than the final a couple of years ago against Juve This time there are no unknowns and we are convinced of. Whereas Xabi Alonso said that it's true, Milan are the favorites, but anything can happen in a final. And the Reds entered the match without conceding a single goal in the last three European matches. And when we look at the two lineups, Ancelotti decided to add an extra striker with Shevchenko, which supports their status as overwhelming favourites. Whereas Rafa Benitez took a chance, and instead of playing Hamann and trying to win the midfield battle, he inserted Harry Kuhl into the starting line-up under the sole striker Baros. And the Aussie struggled with injuries in the last couple of seasons, that's why the decision to start him was a surprise. Fans of both sides arrived in big numbers and travelled long distances
to watch what they hope is an exciting final. history, contesting the showpiece final at the end of another Champions League season.
This is where every player in the world wants to be tonight.

A goal in 50 seconds from Milan. I would you believe it. Paolo Maldini, the skipper, has scored it. Swung in towards Javier. Straight at Dida. Hornet's turned off the line this time by Luis Garcia.
